proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_078 (continuous, range ~30-90) are associated with longer pfs_months. Direction: positive linear effect.

analyses.result_summary: OLS slope of pfs_months on feature_078 = 0.1740 months per unit (SE 0.0005); Pearson r=0.856, p=0.00e+00. PFS rises from ~1.3 mo in lowest quintile of feature_078 to ~6.2 mo in the highest.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the ordinal feature_057 (levels 0/1/2) are associated with shorter pfs_months (negative monotonic effect).

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway over feature_057 levels; stats.linregress(feature_057, pfs)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_057: 0=4.68, 1=3.49, 2=2.38 mo. One-way ANOVA F=4445.9, p=0.00e+00. Linear trend slope=-1.161 mo/level, p=0.00e+00: monotonic decrease with rising feature_057.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_051==1 (binary; prevalence ~55%) have shorter pfs_months than feature_051==0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_051=1: 3.509 mo (n=27481), feature_051=0: 4.031 mo (n=22519); difference=-0.521 mo, Welch t=-28.9, p=1.58e-181.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_013 (continuous, max>3000; plausibly a tumor-burden lab such as PSA) is associated with shorter pfs_months. Use log1p to handle skew.

analyses.result_summary: OLS slope of pfs_months on log1p(feature_013) = -0.3817 mo per log-unit (SE 0.0072); Pearson r=-0.231, p=0.00e+00. Higher feature_013 → shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_009 (continuous, mean ~3.8, range up to 5.5; plausibly a nutritional/inflammatory marker such as albumin) is associated with longer pfs_months. Direction: positive.

analyses.result_summary: Slope = 0.4949 mo per unit feature_009 (SE 0.0179); r=0.123, p=1.47e-166. Higher feature_009 → longer PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_006 (continuous, max ~24; plausibly an enzyme/liver marker) is associated with shorter pfs_months. Direction: negative.

analyses.result_summary: Slope = -0.0749 mo per unit feature_006 (SE 0.0023); r=-0.143, p=9.91e-226. Higher feature_006 → shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_067 (integer 6-10; plausibly Gleason grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months. Expect negative slope.

analyses.result_summary: Slope = -0.0062 mo per integer step (SE 0.0089); r=-0.0031, p=4.86e-01. Mean PFS by feature_067 (6→10): 6:3.71, 7:3.76, 8:3.74, 9:3.74, 10:3.70. Hypothesis NOT supported — feature_067 has essentially no PFS effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across feature_018 categories (asian, black, hispanic, other, white) — i.e., a racial/ethnic disparity in PFS exists.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_018: asian=3.797, black=3.741, hispanic=3.737, other=3.749, white=3.741. One-way ANOVA F=0.56, p=0.689. Max-min between-group spread is 0.060 mo. No meaningful disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across feature_045 categories (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured) — i.e., an insurance-status disparity in PFS exists.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_045: medicaid=3.741, medicare=3.754, private=3.736, uninsured=3.726. F=0.34, p=0.794. Spread 0.028 mo. No meaningful disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After joint adjustment in a single OLS model, feature_078 (positive), feature_057 (negative), feature_051 (negative), log1p(feature_013) (negative), and feature_009 (positive) all retain independent associations with pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + log_feature_013 + feature_009 + feature_006', data=df).fit()

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between feature_078 (continuous) and feature_051 (binary): the slope of pfs_months on feature_078 differs by feature_051 status. Specifically the negative impact of feature_051 narrows or widens with increasing feature_078.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient feature_078:feature_051 = -0.00469 (p=3.297e-07). Significant: per-unit-age PFS gain differs between feature_051 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between feature_057 (ordinal 0/1/2) and feature_051 (binary) on pfs_months: the PFS detriment of feature_051 is amplified at higher feature_057.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_057:feature_051 = +0.0438 (p=7.396e-02). No significant interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The mean pfs_months difference for feature_051==1 vs ==0 is negative within every stratum of feature_057, but the magnitude differs across strata (stratified subgroup test).

analyses.result_summary: Stratum feature_057=0: mean PFS diff (1-0)=-0.559 mo (n1=9704, n0=7888); Welch t=-19.51, p=7.27e-84.
Stratum feature_057=1: mean PFS diff (1-0)=-0.514 mo (n1=13676, n0=11295); Welch t=-21.87, p=5.19e-105.
Stratum feature_057=2: mean PFS diff (1-0)=-0.472 mo (n1=4101, n0=3336); Welch t=-11.59, p=8.89e-31.

proposed_hypotheses.text: log1p(feature_013) retains a significant negative association with pfs_months after adjusting for feature_078 and feature_057.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient on log_feature_013 = -0.3827 mo per log-unit (p=0.00e+00). Effect persists after adjustment for feature_078 and feature_057.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS that includes the strongest main-effect candidates (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, log_feature_013, feature_009, feature_006, feature_109, feature_039, feature_043) explains substantially more variance than feature_078 alone.

analyses.code: compare_f_test of m_big vs m_small (feature_078 only)
analyses.result_summary: R^2 feature_078-only=0.732; full multivariable R^2=0.970. ΔR^2=0.238. Joint F-test for added predictors: F=49763.9, p=0.00e+00, df=8.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS effect of feature_018 (race) is modified by feature_045 (insurance); i.e., a race×insurance interaction term improves the model fit.

analyses.result_summary: Joint F-test for race×insurance interaction: F=0.50, p=0.914, df=12. No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative PFS effect of feature_051 differs across feature_018 (race) categories; a race × feature_051 interaction is detectable.

analyses.result_summary: Joint F=0.41, p=0.803. No heterogeneity by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_109==1 (binary, prevalence ~10%) have longer pfs_months than feature_109==0 patients.
Patients with feature_039==1 (binary, prevalence ~10%) have longer pfs_months than feature_039==0 patients.
Patients with feature_043==1 (binary, prevalence ~20%) have shorter pfs_months than feature_043==0 patients.
Higher feature_038 (continuous, mean ~12.5; plausibly hemoglobin) is associated with longer pfs_months. Direction: positive.

analyses.result_summary: Slope=-0.0115 mo per unit feature_038 (SE 0.0050); r=-0.0102, p=2.25e-02. Significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_078 and pfs_months is non-linear; adding a quadratic term (feature_078**2) significantly improves the model.

analyses.code: compare_f_test linear vs quadratic OLS in feature_078
analyses.result_summary: Quadratic coef = +0.000776 (p=1.86e-112); F-test for adding quadratic: F=510.45, p=1.86e-112. R^2 linear=0.732, quadratic=0.735.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS-shortening effect of feature_057 is modified by feature_078 — specifically, the per-level decrement in pfs_months associated with feature_057 depends on feature_078 (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_078:feature_057 = -0.00981 (p=7.892e-100). Significant: feature_057 effect changes with feature_078.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative PFS effect of higher log1p(feature_013) is modified by feature_051: in feature_051==1 patients the slope is more strongly negative than in feature_051==0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log_feature_013:feature_051 = -0.0102 (p=5.880e-01). No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An omnibus OLS that combines feature_078 (with quadratic), feature_057, feature_051, log_feature_013, feature_009, feature_006, feature_018 (race), and feature_045 (insurance) yields a model dominated by feature_078, feature_057, and feature_051, with race and insurance contributing essentially no explanatory power.

analyses.code: OLS full model + nested F-test removing race+insurance
analyses.result_summary: Full model R^2=0.972; without race+insurance R^2=0.972. F-test for race+insurance block: F=0.07, p=1.000, df=7. Smallest p-values in full model: feature_078 (p=0.00e+00), feature_078_sq (p=0.00e+00), feature_057 (p=0.00e+00), log_feature_013 (p=0.00e+00), feature_009 (p=0.00e+00), feature_006 (p=0.00e+00).
